applied.note: getVideo viewCount inc: .exec() now chained with .catch((e)=>console.error('[video] viewCount inc:', e.message)) — prevents unhandledRejection
updateVideo: 'if (title) video.title = title' -> 'if (title !== undefined)' for consistency (empty string no longer silently rejected)

.lean() added to getCourseVideos and getTopVideos (both read-only, sent directly, no .save()). getVideo left WITHOUT .lean() (doc fields/course used in response, mutation-adjacent flow) — searchVideos already had .lean()

getAllCourses pagination clamp: lim=Math.min(Math.max(1,parseInt(limit)||12),50), pg=Math.max(1,parseInt(page)||1), skip=(pg-1)*lim; query and pagination envelope (page/limit/pages) use clamped values; envelope shape unchanged
getTopCourses limit clamp Math.min(parseInt(limit)||6,20); getRecommendedCourses limit clamp Math.min(parseInt(limit)||4,20). getUserRecommendedCourses already had clamp (Math.min(...||6,20))
getAllCourses, getTopCourses, getRecommendedCourses, getCourse read-only finds already used .lean(); getCourse viewCount inc already had .exec().catch() (BQ-008 equivalent already fixed)

summary: 6 fixes applied across videoController and courseController (BQ-008 viewCount .catch, BQ-015 title !== undefined, BQ-003 getAllCourses pagination clamp, BQ-004 limit clamps on getTopVideos/getTopCourses/getRecommendedCourses, BQ-010/PERF-006 .lean() on getCourseVideos/getTopVideos). courseController .lean() and getCourse .catch() were already in place. getVideo intentionally left without .lean() (response uses doc fields). Response formats and pagination envelope shapes preserved; no ObjectId validation added. node --check passes for both files.
